title: A Global Systematic Review, Meta-analysis and Health Risk Assessment of the Concentration of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) in Bread: A Protocol for a Systematic Review with Meta-analysis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"impix","text":"Background: Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons are formed during various industrial processes and activities, mainly through pyrolysis, especially when organic matter is incompletely combusted. There is evidence that PAHs affect host resistance, humoral immunity, and immune system development. These health problems may occasionally be linked to genotoxicity, chronic intoxication symptoms, and carcinogenic, mutagenic, and teratogenic effects. One of the most common food sources in human nutrition is bread . Bread can be contaminated with PAHs due to contamination of baking ingredients (mainly flour) and the baking process. guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bs9jl","text":"Over 200 organic compounds with fused aromatic rings form the polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons group (1). They can be categorized into light (2-3 rings) and heavy (4-6 rings), with heavy PAHs being more potent and toxic. (2). Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons are formed during various industrial processes and activities, mainly through pyrolysis, especially when organic matter is incompletely combusted. PAHs are highly lipophilic and have low water solubility. They are generally found in air, surface waters, soils, and sediments (1).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6hise","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"i2opb","text":"There is evidence that PAHs affect host resistance, humoral immunity, and immune system development. These health problems may occasionally be linked to genotoxicity, chronic intoxication symptoms, and carcinogenic, mutagenic, and teratogenic effects (3). Also, the World Health Organization (WHO) and the European Food Safety Authority (EFSA) have approved the health risks associated with PAHs (4).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4j0t","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"paxkv","text":"Studies on PAH exposure indicate that food is the primary way that people are exposed to them, and cereals are a major source of exposure (3). Increasing consumption of cereals and cereal-based products in most countries has increased human exposure to PAHs (5). One of the most common food sources in human nutrition is bread (6). It contains proteins, vitamins, lipids, and minerals that are necessary for human nutrition. Bread can be contaminated with PAHs due to contamination of baking ingredients (mainly flour) and the baking process. Thermal treatment temperature also needs to be considered, because it impacts bread contamination levels (7). Although several studies have been conducted to measure the concentrations of PAHs in bread around the world, no systematic review has been conducted. Therefore, the current study will be aimed to investigate the concentration of PAHs in bread on a global scale. Furthermore, it will be estimated that polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons are responsible for the probabilistic health risks due to the consumption of bread contaminated with PAHs.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"rcal3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: This systematic review will adhere to the Preferred Reporting Item for Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analysis Protocols (PRISMA-P), as well as the Cochrane guidelines.

Literature searches will be conducted in Science Direct, PubMed, Web of Science, Scopus, and Google Scholar, will be searched from early to September 2022. Each database will be searched independently, and the reference lists of all studies will be manually searched for relevant literature, which may be omitted during the search. We will use keywords related to the terms “polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons” and “PAHs”. These keywords are then combined with the terms “bread” and “health risk” to be used.
The selection of studies will be made according to the following criteria: Full-text available
Published text in the English language
Reporting the concentration of PAHs in bread
Covers appropriate statistical data such as sample size, mean and standard deviation.
Experimental studies, review articles, relevant conference papers, case reports, books, and clinical trial studies will be excluded.

The quality of the studies included in the research will be evaluated by 2 independent reviewers using the Newcastle-Ottawa Scale (8). Six items on this scale can be answered with either a "no" or "yes" response, which makes up a general score for each study.

Each study in the review will be independently extracted using a standard data extraction table, which includes publication year, author, PAHs level, bread type, oven type, country, and attributes of the method used.

PAH concentrations in bread will be estimated using mean values ​​and standard errors (SE). This study will be divided into subgroups according to the geographical area of ​​origin, the type of bread, the type of oven, and the quality of paper. STATA 14.0 (Texas, USA) will be used for data analysis.
Main objective: The main objective of our study will be to evaluate variations in the concentrations of PAH compounds in bread samples.

Secondary objective: Our secondary objective will be to evaluate dietary intake and health risk assessment of PAHs due to the consumption of bread. Also, will be Investigating the effect of the type of oven, type of bread, geographical location, and quality of articles on the concentration of PAH compound in bread.

A systematic review with meta-analysis to assess the health risks associated with PAH levels in bread will be conducted. This study will be investigated all bread consumed on a global scale. In order to be able to compare the amount of PAHs in bread, the type of bread, the type of oven, and the geographical origin will be used to determine which Factor is most effective in raising PAH concentration. In addition, as another consequence, human health risk assessment of these compounds will also be conducted. As this study is a meta-analysis, so we will discuss the full text of the studies that have been published. For this reason, studies that are in the submission phase and those that do not have a full text will not be considered.
